questions.question: What is the current pass rate for backend integration tests according to the document?
Which testing framework is used for backend integration tests?
What are the three main environment variables required for backend integration tests, and what do they specify?
questions.reference_answer: SUPABASE_URL (local Supabase API URL, default http://127.0.0.1:54321), SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (public anonymous key for RLS-protected operations), and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (admin key for bypassing RLS)

questions.question: How many test suites are included in the backend integration tests, and what is the total number of test cases?
What is the purpose of using data-testid attributes in E2E tests, and what is the alternative selector strategy that should be avoided?
questions.reference_answer: data-testid attributes provide reliable, stable element targeting. The alternative selectors that should be avoided are fragile selectors like getByRole and getByText which can break when UI text changes

questions.question: According to the document, what is the main reason E2E tests are non-blocking in CI rather than blocking?
questions.reference_answer: E2E tests require full infrastructure (Supabase, Redis, frontend dev servers), have longer execution times, may require fixes after UI changes, and critical bugs are caught by integration tests plus manual QA

questions.question: How many Player Web E2E tests are currently passing, and how many are skipped?
questions.reference_answer: 30 passing tests, 8 skipped tests (7 for favorites feature not implemented, 1 for session refresh requiring investigation)

questions.question: What is the naming convention for test IDs in the Player Web E2E tests to prevent Playwright strict mode violations?
questions.reference_answer: Card elements use a prefix (game-card-title, card-play-button, card-demo-button) while modal elements use standard names (game-title, play-button, demo-button) to distinguish between card and modal contexts

questions.question: How many total tests are included in the Operator Web E2E test suite across all test files?
questions.reference_answer: 138 total tests distributed across 6 spec files

questions.question: What are the admin and regular user test credentials provided for Operator Web E2E tests?
questions.reference_answer: Admin credentials: admin@test.local / admin123456, Regular user: user@test.local / user123456

questions.question: Which two critical issues are documented as known issues in Operator Web E2E tests, and what is their priority status?
questions.reference_answer: UI Text Mismatches (HIGH priority - tests expect 'Sign In' heading but UI shows 'Operator Login') and Playwright webServer timeout (HIGH priority - config checks non-existent endpoint, should use specific function URL)

questions.question: What command is used to run backend integration tests with a filter to match specific test cases?
questions.reference_answer: deno test --allow-all --env-file=.env.test --filter "[test case name]" functions/__tests__/[test file].ts

questions.question: How is Playwright configured for E2E tests regarding retries and artifacts?
questions.reference_answer: Retries are set to 0 (no retry masking). Artifacts include screenshots, traces, and videos on failure. Browser is Chromium (headless by default), workers are 1 in CI and 4 locally, with base URLs for Player and Operator apps set to port 3001

questions.question: What percentage of Authentication tests pass in Player Web E2E tests, and what is the reason for the skipped test?
questions.reference_answer: 89% (8/9 passing). The skipped test is for session refresh which requires profile API investigation after page reload

questions.question: What are the differences between how backend integration tests are run in local development versus how they must be configured in CI?
questions.reference_answer: Locally: tests use local Supabase instance started with 'supabase start', environment variables come from .env.test file. In CI: uses GitHub Actions secrets (UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN must be configured), while SUPABASE credentials are auto-configured from workflow execution

questions.question: How do the selector strategies differ between Player Web and Operator Web E2E tests, and what is the reasoning behind each approach?
questions.reference_answer: Player Web uses data-testid attributes (stable against UI text changes), while Operator Web uses semantic selectors like getByRole, getByLabel, getByText (preferred for accessibility and maintainability) with data-testid as fallback for complex cases

questions.question: What are the four main test categories covered in Operator Web E2E tests, and what specific workflows does Games Management validation include?
questions.reference_answer: Four categories: Authentication (24 tests), Games Management (28 tests), Configurations Management (22 tests), and Players Management (26 tests). Games Management includes: Display (list view, columns, search), CRUD operations, Status Toggle with optimistic updates, Search & Filter (by name, status, provider), Pagination, and Cache Updates

questions.question: According to the troubleshooting section, what are the three most common issues when running E2E tests, and what is the command to verify Supabase status?
questions.reference_answer: Common issues: (1) Supabase not running, (2) Port 3001 already in use, (3) Test timeout errors. The command to verify Supabase status is 'cd apps/api && supabase status'
